texto: Nothing seems to me more tragic than the pressures we put on artists to do what we think we'll like, and our refusal to prize what is most ornery and special about them.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Marcia B. Siegel: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “Nothing seems to me more tragic than the pressures we put on artists to do what we think we'll li…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, art, artist pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de artist.
